uid.description: The user's unique ID from Firebase Authentication.

photoURL.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

definition.description: Stores public user profile information. The document ID must match the user's Firebase Auth UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure is designed to be simple and secure. The primary collection is `/users`, where each document's ID is the Firebase Authentication UID of the user. This enforces a strict one-to-one relationship between an authentication identity and a user profile document, enabling strong, path-based security rules. All user-specific data that is not collaborative can be stored in sub-collections under the user's document, ensuring data isolation and straightforward ownership rules.
